title: Geopolitics and International Security: Research & Analysis | CSIS
text:
Geopolitics and International Security

Prev

Global Governance and Institutions

Allies and Partners

Next

CSIS experts utilize deep expertise and global relationships to help policymakers in navigate the evolving challenges to U.S. global leadership. Their work examines the key political, economic, security, and societal trends shaping the future of global governance and institutions.

Image

Photo: ALFREDO ESTRELLA/AFP/Getty Images

Fortress North America: Mexico’s Role in Securing the Region’s Economic Future

The 2026 USMCA review is the first test of whether the United States, Mexico, and Canada can turn economic security into a regional framework. Mexico is the hinge, as the top U.S. trading partner, but is also importing from China at a 14-to-1 ratio.

Report
by
Diego Marroquín Bitar

The Last Line of Defense

The Surprising Truth About How Americans See NATO, China, and Iran

On this episode, host Seth Jones discusses areas of bipartisan support for global engagement, including NATO, the threat posed by China, support for Ukraine, and more.

Podcast Episode
by
Seth G. Jones

— July 2, 2026

Into Africa

Old Playbooks, New Threats: Africa’s Evolving Security Landscape

In the season two premiere of Into Africa, Dr. Olajumoke Ayandele, Clinical Assistant Professor at New York University, and Peter Bofin, Senior Analyst, Southeast Africa at ACLED, join Oge to unpack this evolving threat landscape. Together, they examine how technology is reshaping how armed groups recruit, finance, and operate. 

Podcast Episode
by
Oge Onubogu

— July 2, 2026

Image

Southeast Asia Radio

Southeast Asian Responses to U.S.-China Conflict with Natalie Sambhi and Bich Tran

This week, Greg was joined by Natalie Sambhi and Bich Tran to discuss their new report on the factors that would shape Southeast Asia responses to potential U.S.-China conflict. Lauren and Japhet cover the latest from Vietnam’s bamboo diplomacy to the latest updates on scam centers.

The Eurofile

Europe Will Survive; But Will It Prosper? feat. David Marsh

Max sits down with David Marsh, co-founder and chairman of the OMFIF economic research group and former Financial Times Europe editor, to discuss his new book Can Europe Survive? The Story of a Continent in a Fractured World.

Max Bergmann

The Impossible State

What is North Korea learning from Russia's war in Ukraine?

Dr. Victor Cha, Ms. Kate Bondar, and Mr. Bruce Klingner discussed how the Ukraine war is reshaping warfare, what North Korea may learn from Russia, and the implications for Korean Peninsula security.

Fortress North America: Mexico’s Role in Securing the Region’s Economic Future

The 2026 USMCA review is the first test of whether the United States, Mexico, and Canada can turn economic security into a regional framework. Mexico is the hinge, as the top U.S. trading partner, but is also importing from China at a 14-to-1 ratio.

Report
by
Diego Marroquín Bitar

— July 1, 2026

Russian Blood and Treasure: The Ballooning Costs of Putin’s War

Russia has suffered approximately 1.4 million casualties and 450,000 fatalities in Ukraine for only marginal territorial gains, according to new CSIS data. In addition, Ukraine has conducted deep strikes into Russian territory, including with AI-enabled drones.

Brief
by

Seth G. Jones
and
Riley McCabe

— July 1, 2026

Don’t Let the USMCA Drift Away

On July 1, North America’s trade pact comes up for review. The real risk isn’t collapse, but rather the slow, expensive erosion already underway.

Commentary
by
Diego Marroquín Bitar

— June 30, 2026
